passages.explanation: Jesus calls his twelve disciples and gives them authority over unclean spirits and to heal every disease and sickness. The twelve are: Simon Peter and Andrew, James and John sons of Zebedee, Philip, Bartholomew, Thomas, Matthew the tax collector, James son of Alphaeus, Thaddaeus, Simon the Zealot, and Judas Iscariot who betrayed him. The list is deliberately diverse — a tax collector and a zealot on the same team would have been politically explosive. Jesus doesn't build a movement of like-minded people; he builds one of surrendered ones.

Jesus sends the twelve out with specific instructions: don't go to the Gentiles or Samaritans. Go instead to the lost sheep of the house of Israel. Preach that the Kingdom of Heaven is at hand. Heal the sick, raise the dead, cleanse the lepers, cast out demons. Freely you received, freely give. Don't take gold, silver, or copper in your belts — no bag, no extra tunic, no sandals, no staff. The worker is worthy of his food. When you enter a town, find someone worthy and stay there. If a house is worthy, let your peace come on it. If not, let your peace return to you. If anyone won't receive you, shake off the dust of your feet as you leave. It will be more tolerable for Sodom and Gomorrah on judgement day than for that town. The mission is urgent, stripped-down, and dependent. Jesus sends them out with authority but without resources — the provision will come through the mission itself.

I'm sending you out as sheep among wolves — be wise as serpents and innocent as doves. Beware of men: they'll deliver you to councils and flog you in synagogues. You'll be brought before governors and kings for my sake, as a testimony to them and to the Gentiles. When they deliver you up, don't be anxious about what to say — what you speak will be given to you in that hour, for it's not you speaking but the Spirit of your Father. Brother will deliver up brother to death, and a father his child. Children will rise up against parents and have them put to death. You'll be hated by all for my name's sake. But whoever endures to the end will be saved. When they persecute you in one city, flee to the next. You won't have gone through all the cities of Israel before the Son of Man comes. Jesus doesn't promise safety — he promises significance. The persecution isn't a surprise; it's part of the briefing.

A disciple is not above his teacher, nor a servant above his master. If they called the head of the house Beelzebul, how much more the members of his household! Don't fear them — nothing is covered that won't be revealed. What I tell you in the dark, speak in the light. Don't fear those who kill the body but can't kill the soul. Rather, fear him who can destroy both soul and body in Gehenna. Aren't two sparrows sold for a penny? Not one of them falls apart from your Father's will. Even the hairs of your head are numbered. Don't be afraid — you're worth more than many sparrows. Everyone who confesses me before men, I'll confess before my Father. Whoever denies me before men, I'll deny before my Father. Fear of God displaces fear of people. If the one who counts sparrows is watching, persecution can't touch you in any ultimate sense.

Don't think I came to send peace on the earth. I didn't come to send peace but a sword. I came to set a man against his father, a daughter against her mother, a daughter-in-law against her mother-in-law. A man's foes will be those of his own household. Whoever loves father or mother more than me isn't worthy of me. Whoever loves son or daughter more than me isn't worthy of me. Whoever doesn't take his cross and follow after me isn't worthy of me. Whoever finds his life will lose it, and whoever loses his life for my sake will find it. The cross reference here predates the crucifixion — the disciples would have understood it as a Roman instrument of execution. Jesus is saying: following me may cost you everything, including family and life.

Whoever receives you receives me, and whoever receives me receives him who sent me. Whoever receives a prophet in the name of a prophet will receive a prophet's reward. Whoever receives a righteous man in the name of a righteous man will receive a righteous man's reward. Whoever gives even a cup of cold water to one of these little ones in the name of a disciple will by no means lose his reward. The mission isn't just about the messengers — it's about everyone who supports them. Even the smallest act of hospitality toward Jesus' people counts.
